name: Anthurium luxurians × forgetii
origin: Horticultural hybrid; derived from tropical American parentage
aboutText: Anthurium luxurians × forgetii is a carefully crafted horticultural hybrid that merges two highly sought Colombian and Panamanian species with complementary traits. The luxurians parent contributes its celebrated bullate, corrugated surface texture — that extraordinary quilted quality that resembles hammered dark metal — while the forgetii parent shapes the leaf form toward the broader, near-circular, closed-sinus silhouette it is famous for. The result is a compact, self-heading plant with broadly rounded cordate leaves showing a reduced sinus and subtle but distinct quilted texture on a deep, rich green surface. Among the bullate-forgetii crosses, this is considered one of the most successful combinations, producing offspring that are visually interesting from the texture of luxurians and architecturally clean from the form of forgetii.

morphology.leafShape: Broadly rounded cordate with reduced sinus, subtle bullate texture
morphology.venation: Subtle pale venation, overshadowed by bullate texture

propagation.notes: Root in a closed high-humidity environment. Mature specimens may produce basal offshoots that can be carefully divided. Patience is essential — establishment is slow.
